title: Reverse Proxies, Forwarded Trust, Message Framing, and Smuggling Defense · Go Build Room
summary: A server pairing session predicts request, handler, dependency, response, and cleanup transitions before building the first proxy rewrite and parser-consistency threat model for reverse proxies, forwarded trust, message framing, and smuggling defense. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of proxy rewrite and parser-consistency threat model while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, an…
objectives: Trace downstream and upstream connections, hop-by-hop versus end-to-end fields, termination points, authority, scheme, client address, and failure ownership.
Use ReverseProxy.Rewrite with separate inbound and outbound requests, SetURL and reviewed SetXForwarded behavior, and reject deprecated Director trust assumptions.
Strip untrusted forwarding fields, rebuild them only at trusted hops, constrain backend targets, remove hop-by-hop state, and prevent header spoofing.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Proxy Hop Origin Model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop team handles case 4669 in a recovery rehearsal with an ambiguous database commit. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject ambiguous framing and deprecated Director-style proxy mutation; then drain the old revision while a canary fails its changed-case invariant. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Go server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Proxy Hop Origin Model.

For Go server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Proxy Hop Origin Model, keep Proxy Hop Origin Model connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

steps.title: Go server Defend the transferred model · Workshop Proxy Query Response Defense
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Proxy Query Response Defense: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop team handles case 4678 in a privacy-sensitive records API using conditional requests. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject ambiguous framing and deprecated Director-style proxy mutation; then make specification and runtime disagree on one nullable field and block release. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Handle unparsable query parameters, backend timeouts, bounded bodies, ModifyResponse, ErrorHandler, streaming, Alt-Svc, and sanitized failure evidence.

Evidence target: Go server Defend the transferred model · Workshop Proxy Query Response Defense.
